description: React + TypeScript SPA that replaces MeOS's Win32/GDI desktop GUI. Communicates exclusively through the JSON REST API. Standalone project in src/ui/web/ with zero dependency on the legacy code/ directory.

userStories.title: Project scaffolding with Vite, React 18, and TypeScript
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project skeleton so that all subsequent stories have a working build environment.
acceptanceCriteria: src/ui/web/ contains a working Vite + React 18 + TypeScript project
package.json includes react, react-dom, react-router-dom, typescript, vite, vitest, @testing-library/react, tailwindcss, and shadcn/ui (or @radix-ui) dependencies
tsconfig.json has strict mode enabled
vite.config.ts configures API proxy to http://localhost:2009 for /api paths
vite.config.ts sets production build output to dist/ directory
.gitignore excludes node_modules, dist, and build artifacts
index.html exists with root mount point
src/main.tsx renders a minimal React app
Tailwind CSS is configured and working
npm run build succeeds without errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: App shell with sidebar navigation and routing
userStories.description: As a user, I want a navigation sidebar and tab-based routing so that I can switch between competition management views.
acceptanceCriteria: App.tsx renders a layout with a persistent sidebar/navigation and a content area
React Router is configured with routes: /competition, /classes, /courses, /controls, /clubs, /runners, /teams, /results, /startlist
Each route renders a placeholder page component (separate file per page in src/pages/)
Navigation highlights the active route
Root path / redirects to /competition
Layout is responsive: sidebar collapses to a hamburger menu at widths below 768px
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: API client layer with TypeScript types and React hooks
userStories.description: As a developer, I need typed API client functions and React hooks so that pages can fetch and mutate data through the REST API.
acceptanceCriteria: src/api/types.ts defines TypeScript interfaces for: Competition, Class, Course, Control, Club, Runner, Team, Result, StartListEntry
src/api/client.ts exports typed fetch-based functions for all CRUD endpoints: GET/POST /api/v1/{entity}, GET/PUT/DELETE /api/v1/{entity}/{id}
src/api/client.ts also exports functions for: GET /api/v1/results, GET /api/v1/startlist, POST /api/v1/cards, POST /api/v1/runners/{id}/status
src/hooks/ contains custom React hooks (e.g., useClubs, useRunners) that wrap the API client with state management (loading, error, data)
API client handles errors consistently and returns typed responses
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Mock API with MSW for frontend-only development
userStories.description: As a developer, I need mock API handlers so that I can develop and test the frontend without a running C++ backend.
acceptanceCriteria: MSW (Mock Service Worker) is installed and configured
Mock handlers exist for all CRUD endpoints (clubs, controls, courses, classes, runners, teams)
Mock handlers exist for: GET /api/v1/competitions, PUT /api/v1/competitions, GET /api/v1/results, GET /api/v1/startlist
Each entity has realistic seed data (at least 5 items per entity type)
Mock handlers support GET (list + single), POST (create), PUT (update), DELETE operations with in-memory storage
MSW is activated in development mode via src/mocks/browser.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Reusable data table component with sorting, filtering, and pagination
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable table component so that all entity list views share consistent behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/DataTable.tsx accepts typed column definitions and data array as props
Table supports column sorting (click header to toggle asc/desc)
Table supports text filtering/search across columns
Table supports pagination with configurable page size
Table renders responsively (horizontal scroll on small screens)
Table rows support an onClick handler for row selection
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Reusable form and dialog components with validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need reusable form components so that all CRUD forms have consistent validation and layout.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/ contains form primitives: FormField, FormInput, FormSelect, SearchableSelect
FormField displays label, input, and validation error message
SearchableSelect supports typeahead filtering of options
src/components/FormDialog.tsx renders a modal dialog for create/edit forms with save and cancel buttons
src/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx renders a confirmation dialog for delete operations
Form validation displays inline error messages for required fields
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified existing components and ensured they work with react-hook-form and zod.

userStories.title: Competition page
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to view and edit competition settings so that I can configure the event.
acceptanceCriteria: Competition page at /competition displays competition details in a form
Form fields include: name, date, organizer, and other competition properties
Save button sends PUT /api/v1/competitions to update settings
Form validation shows errors for required fields
Success/error feedback is shown after save
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Classes page with CRUD
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to manage classes so that runners can be grouped by category.
acceptanceCriteria: Classes page at /classes displays all classes in a DataTable
Table columns include: name, course assignment, start method
Create button opens a FormDialog to add a new class (POST /api/v1/classes)
Clicking a row opens the edit form (PUT /api/v1/classes/{id})
Delete button with ConfirmDialog removes a class (DELETE /api/v1/classes/{id})
Course assignment uses a dropdown populated from GET /api/v1/courses
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Courses page with CRUD and control sequence
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to manage courses with their control sequences so that I can define the route runners must follow.
acceptanceCriteria: Courses page at /courses displays all courses in a DataTable
Table columns include: name, length, number of controls
Create/edit form includes fields: name, length, and a control sequence builder
Control sequence builder allows adding, removing, and reordering controls from the available controls list
CRUD operations use the /api/v1/courses endpoints
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Controls page with CRUD
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to manage controls so that I can define the checkpoints on courses.
acceptanceCriteria: Controls page at /controls displays all controls in a DataTable
Table columns include: code/number, type/status
Create button opens a FormDialog to add a new control
Edit and delete operations work via /api/v1/controls endpoints
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Clubs page with CRUD
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to manage clubs so that runners can be associated with their organizations.
acceptanceCriteria: Clubs page at /clubs displays all clubs in a DataTable
Table columns include: name and any other club properties
Create button opens a FormDialog to add a new club
Edit and delete operations work via /api/v1/clubs endpoints
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Runners page with CRUD, search, and filtering
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to manage runners with search and filtering so that I can efficiently handle large competitor lists.
acceptanceCriteria: Runners page at /runners displays all runners in a DataTable
Table columns include: name, club, class, start time, card number, status
Search/filter supports filtering by name, club, and class
Club and class fields in create/edit form use SearchableSelect dropdowns
CRUD operations use the /api/v1/runners endpoints
Table uses virtualized scrolling or pagination to handle 1000+ runners efficiently
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Teams page with CRUD and member management
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to manage teams and their members so that I can organize relay and team competitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Teams page at /teams displays all teams in a DataTable
Table columns include: name, club, class, number of members
Create/edit form includes fields: name, club, class, and a member list
Member list allows adding and removing runners from the team
CRUD operations use the /api/v1/teams endpoints
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Runner import from CSV and IOF XML
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to import runners from CSV and IOF XML files so that I can quickly populate the competitor list.
acceptanceCriteria: Import button on the Runners page opens an import dialog
Dialog supports file upload for CSV and IOF XML formats
CSV parsing happens client-side (using Papa Parse or similar)
IOF XML files are uploaded to the backend for server-side parsing
Preview step shows imported runners before confirmation with conflict detection (new vs. existing)
Invalid data rows are highlighted with error messages
Confirming the import sends the data to POST /api/v1/runners
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Bulk operations on runners
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to select multiple runners and perform bulk actions so that I can efficiently manage large competitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Runner table has checkbox selection for individual and select-all
Bulk action toolbar appears when runners are selected, showing the count of selected runners
Bulk assign class: dropdown to pick a class, applies to all selected runners
Bulk assign start times: triggers a draw/assignment for selected runners
Bulk status change: dropdown to set status for all selected runners
Each bulk operation shows a confirmation dialog before executing
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Results and start list views
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to view results and start lists per class so that I can monitor competition progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Results page at /results displays results grouped by class
Results include split times with standard orienteering formatting (time behind leader)
Start list page at /startlist displays start lists grouped by class
Class selector allows filtering results/start list to a specific class
Print-friendly CSS styles via @media print produce clean output
Data is fetched from GET /api/v1/results and GET /api/v1/startlist
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Live results with auto-refresh
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want results to auto-update so that I can monitor the competition in real time.
acceptanceCriteria: Results page has a 'Live' toggle button to enable auto-refresh
When enabled, results are re-fetched via polling at a configurable interval (default 10 seconds)
Recently changed rows are highlighted with a fade animation
Refresh interval is configurable via a settings control on the results page
A visual indicator shows when live mode is active and the time since last refresh
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Results and start list export
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to export results and start lists so that I can share them externally.
acceptanceCriteria: Results page has export buttons for CSV and IOF XML formats
Start list page has export buttons for CSV and IOF XML formats
CSV export is generated client-side and triggers a file download
IOF XML export calls a backend endpoint and triggers a file download
Export filenames include the competition name and date
Typecheck passes
